extraction.one_line_summary: GAVIRIA Fernando won Stage 4 sprint with single leadout man RICHEZE Max after Quick-Step abandoned full train control, beating SAGAN and GREIPEL via left-side wind-sheltered slingshot.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step abandoned dominant train tactics from Stage 1, relying on single leadout RICHEZE exploiting left-side wind shelter for slingshot effect.
Dimension Data squandered CAVENDISH's position by chasing breakaway for the peloton rather than preserving leadout train for final kilometer.
GAVIRIA's second acceleration capability at 500m uphill into headwind (36mph average) proves decisive against single-kick sprinters like GREIPEL.
EF Education First successfully protected URAN's GC after crash under 5km by teammate MARTINEZ surrendering his bike immediately.

rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: positive: Two-kick sprint capability uniquely effective in uphill headwind finishes, maintains speed where early launchers fade
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Perfectly positioned by RENSHAW at 1km but lacked legs to capitalize, suggesting diminished top-end power
rider_signals.GREIPEL Andre: positive: Physical contact 50-75m out shows aggressive positioning shift from typical 'gentle giant' approach, nearly secured victory
rider_signals.URAN Rigoberto: neutral: Quick bike-swap scramble with EF squad saved GC, but vulnerability to crashes within 5km reveals team positioning issue
rider_signals.RICHEZE Max: positive: Single-leadout wind-sheltered approach more effective than multi-rider trains in crosswind sprint finishes
